annotations.label: Parabola as focus–directrix locus

anchor.snippet: The parabola is the locus of points in that plane that are equidistant from the directrix and the focus

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of parabola, focus, or directrix, so the focus–directrix locus is not formalized.

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of conic sections (the only 'conic'/'cone' files concern convex cones and enriched category theory).

annotations.note: Parabolas are undefined in Mathlib, so identifying a quadratic graph as a parabola is not formalized.

annotations.label: Parabola (locus definition)

annotations.label: Similar objects in the Euclidean plane
